insight: For Belgium, hydration breaks in the 2026 World Cup have had a mixed impact on their momentum. On average, they tend to lose a bit of momentum after a break, with their performance declining by 0.050 minutes per minute of play. However, the numbers suggest that when Belgium is not pressing before a break, they are actually able to gain some momentum afterwards, with their performance improving by 0.100 minutes per minute of play, whereas when they are pressing before a break, their momentum tends to decline significantly, by 0.200 minutes per minute of play, and they have yet to successfully maintain their momentum in such situations. This indicates that Belgium may need to adjust their strategy when pressing before a hydration break to avoid losing their edge.
